# agent.opensverige — full context

Sveriges öppna AI-agent-readiness scanner. Scannar webbplatser för hur väl
de är förberedda för AI-agenter (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, Perplexity).

## What it is

A scanner that checks 17 technical signals across three categories:

- **Discovery (7 checks)** — can agents find and read the site?
  robots_ok, crawler_access, sitemap_exists, llms_txt, llms_full_txt,
  markdown_negotiation, ssr_content
- **Compliance (3 checks)** — does it meet EU regulatory requirements?
  privacy_automation, cookie_bot_handling, ai_content_marking
- **Builder (7 checks)** — can devs/agents build against it?
  api_exists, openapi_spec, api_docs, mcp_server, mcp_well_known,
  mcp_server_card, sandbox_available

Each check returns pass/fail with severity (critical/important/info).
Total score becomes a green/yellow/red badge.

## Why we built it

- Cloudflare's isitagentready.com (April 17, 2026) is global English with
  zero EU compliance checks. We're the Swedish/Nordic alternative.
- 300+ builders in our Discord need a scanner that respects EU jurisdiction.
- EU AI Act Art. 50 (effective Aug 2, 2026) requires AI-disclosure
  patterns no other scanner implements yet.

## How it works

1. User submits a domain via web UI or POST /api/v1/scan
2. Pipeline (lib/scan/pipeline.ts) fans out parallel HTTP probes:
   - robots.txt, sitemap variants (.xml, _index, wp-sitemap, .php), llms.txt
   - Builder paths (~30 well-known API + dev-portal paths)
   - Compliance paths (privacy-policy, gdpr, cookies)
   - 3x crawler-UA probes (ClaudeBot, GPTBot, PerplexityBot)
   - .well-known/mcp + mcp/server-card.json
   - Markdown content negotiation probe
3. Firecrawl renders JS-heavy pages (homepage + dev portals)
4. Exa semantic search finds dev portals at non-standard locations
5. Anthropic Claude generates: industry classification, summary,
   3 agent-suggestions
6. Results persist to Supabase (eu-west-2 London)
7. Per EU AI Act Art. 50: every response includes ai_disclosure
   { ai_generated: true, model: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5",
     fields: ["summary", "industry", "agent_suggestions"] }

## Tech stack

- Next.js 15 (app router) on Vercel
- TypeScript strict mode
- Tailwind CSS 3 + shadcn/ui (Radix primitives)
- Supabase Postgres for scan history + rate limiting (eu-west-2)
- Anthropic Claude for AI summaries
- Firecrawl for JS rendering
- Exa for semantic dev-portal discovery
- pg_cron for 90-day retention enforcement (EU-native)

## API

Public REST API at /api/v1. OpenAPI 3.1 spec at /openapi.yaml.

POST /api/v1/scan — synchronous, returns full result in 10-30s
GET /api/v1/scan/{id} — lookup by UUID with ETag/304 support

Authentication: Authorization: Bearer osv_(test|live)_<24chars>
Rate limits per tier:
  hobby: 1/min, 15/month
  builder: 1/min, 300/month
  pro: 2/min, 2000/month

Alpha: invite-only via Discord. Stage 2B (async + webhooks) on roadmap.

## Licensing

Source-available under FSL-1.1-MIT (Functional Source License). Two-year
prohibition on competing managed services; auto-converts to MIT after.
Contact info@opensverige.se for commercial licensing.

## Privacy + retention

Hashed IP (HMAC-SHA256 + server-held pepper) for rate limiting only.
Scan results + hashed IP retained 90 days, then auto-deleted via pg_cron.
Anthropic processes domain names + public scan content (no IPs);
migrating to AWS Bedrock Frankfurt before Aug 2, 2026.

## Compliance

- GDPR: Art. 6(1)(f) legitimate interest. Documented Transfer Impact
  Assessment per EDPB 01/2020. Register of Processing Activities (Art.
  30) maintained internally.
- EU AI Act Art. 50: machine-readable disclosure on all AI-generated
  output via ai_disclosure field. Deployer (us) vs Provider (Anthropic)
  delineation per articles 3(3)/(4).
- DSAR endpoint at /api/dsar with 30-day SLA per Art. 12(3).
- Sub-processor list at /legal/subprocessors.

## Methodology in detail

See https://github.com/opensverige/agent-scan/blob/main/docs/SCANNER-METHODOLOGY.md
for full per-check description, sources, and limitations.
